summary: A solid Charlie Miller soundboard capture that’s a bit bass‑heavy, but the June 1992 show shines with an inspired So Many Roads and a stellar China→Rider sequence.
review: The recording is a typical Miller board – generally clear and well‑balanced yet burdened by overly strong low frequencies that can strain speakers. Musically the concert is highly praised: fans highlight So Many Roads, the blazing China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider medley, a memorable Dark Star and an energetic Crazy Fingers→Corrina→Drumz stretch. Overall the performance is regarded as one of the stronger shows of that summer tour.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and rave about the set, while recording comments note only a low‑end muddiness issue; weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear overall mix with crisp mids/highs; however the lows are excessively heavy/muddy, causing speaker strain for some listeners.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans describe the concert as high‑energy with smooth transitions – a strong opener, an explosive second set featuring tight segues (Crazy Fingers→Corrina→Drumz) and a powerful closer.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals described as strong and confident (especially on "Don't Ease Me In"), guitar tone praised; minor lyric slip noted during Crazy Fingers.
band_member_comments.Phil: Stepped up to the mic for So Many Roads, adding vocal variety that listeners appreciated.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboards are called “smokin’” and add a rich texture throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill (drums) highlighted in the Drumz segment; rhythm section praised for tight, energetic playing.
